title: Week of Feb 24 - Mar 1, 2026
summary: Instrument watchlist with live IB quotes, custom charting system, P&L history tab, and commission capture fix.

features.title: Instrument Watchlist
features.summary: Create named watchlists with live IB price data for stocks, indices, and futures on the Charts page
features.description:
A new Watchlist tab on the Charts page lets you create and manage named lists of instruments with live price data from Interactive Brokers.

Key features:
- Create multiple named watchlists (e.g., "Core Positions", "Futures Watch")
- Add stocks, indices, and futures with SEC EDGAR ticker autocomplete
- Live price table: last price, change, change %, volume, bid/ask — all from IB
- 8-second auto-refresh with smart polling (pauses when tab is hidden)
- Green/red color coding for positive/negative changes
- ColumnChooser for user-controlled column visibility
- Full comfort mode support via d-table fluid scaling
- Rename, delete, and switch between watchlists
- Smart futures support: auto-detects exchange from symbol (no manual dropdown), accepts both bare roots (ES) and full contracts (SIK6, CLJ6)
- Futures contract month display: shows embedded month code for specific contracts (SIK6 → K6) or derived front month for bare roots (ES → H6)
- Reorder tickers with up/down arrows on each row — order persists across sessions
- Adaptive number formatting for mobile: prices use fewer decimals at higher magnitudes

The watchlist data is stored per-user in the database. Quotes are fetched in batch through the existing IB relay infrastructure — same pipeline used by the trading tools.

features.title: Custom Charting System
features.summary: TradingView-quality candlestick charts with BMC signal overlays and trade entry/exit markers in a drag-and-drop widget grid
features.description:
A new Charts tab in M&A Options brings professional-grade candlestick charts powered by lightweight-charts v5 and Polygon.io market data.

Key features:
- Full OHLCV candlestick + volume histogram charts for any ticker
- Timeframe selector: 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 1D
- BMC signal overlay: probability markers (arrows with percentages) and signal histogram showing call/put direction with color intensity
- Trade markers: entry arrows (green BUY) and exit arrows (colored by P&L) with trailing stop, stop loss, and profit target labels
- Drag-and-drop widget grid with signal panel and positions panel
- Layout persistence via localStorage
- Independent polling: 30s bars, 5s signals, 10s execution (all pause when tab is hidden)

Architecturally, charts are completely isolated from the trading engine. They read from Polygon.io via a server-side API proxy and overlay signals from the existing BMC/execution polling endpoints. If charts crash, trading is unaffected.

features.title: P&L History Tab
features.summary: Historical algo trade performance with grouped aggregations, position detail, and expandable fill rows
features.description:
A new P&L History tab on the IB Trading Tools page shows historical algorithmic trade performance from PostgreSQL.

Key features:
- Summary totals bar: total trades, win rate, gross P&L, commission, and net P&L
- Group-by selector: toggle between date, symbol, or model version aggregation
- Clickable summary rows filter the positions table to that group
- Position detail table with ColumnChooser (14 columns, user-configurable)
- Expandable rows: click any position to see individual fills with time, level, qty, price, P&L %, commission, and exchange
- Date range and status filters (closed/active/all)
- Green/red P&L coloring, exit reason badges (risk/expired/manual), and level badges (entry/trailing/expired)
- Full comfort mode support via d-table-wrap fluid scaling

All data comes from the deployed P&L history API (v1.16.0) — zero backend changes needed.

features.title: Commission & P&L Capture Fix
features.summary: Fixed two bugs preventing commission and realized P&L from being stored in the trade history pipeline
features.description:
Two bugs in the execution engine prevented commission data from being captured and stored:

1. The fill persistence code read execId from IB's orderStatus callback, which never includes it — only the execDetails callback carries execId. Every fill was stored with an empty exec_id, so the deferred commission lookup never matched.

2. IB's commissionReport callback was routed through the exec_details_listeners with order_id=0. The engine's guard clause dropped these events since order_id 0 was never in the strategy map.

The fix adds a three-path commission capture system:
- Event-driven: commission events are now classified as a distinct event type and routed directly via an exec_id → position map
- execDetails enrichment: when execDetails arrives, the engine retroactively sets exec_id on the fill and triggers a deferred commission poll
- Fallback polling: the existing deferred poll now works correctly with real exec_ids (timeout increased from 2s to 5s)

This means all future trades will have commission costs and IB-reported realized P&L captured in the P&L History tab. Agent v1.17.1.

features.title: Futures Charts via IB
features.summary: Candlestick charts now support futures instruments using IB historical data
features.description:
Charts automatically detect futures tickers (ES, NQ, CL, GC, etc.) and fetch OHLCV bar data from Interactive Brokers instead of Polygon. Supports all standard timeframes (1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 1D) with the same 30-second auto-refresh.

Futures symbols are auto-detected from a known list including equity indices (ES, NQ, YM, RTY), energy (CL, NG), metals (GC, SI), treasuries (ZB, ZN), and grains (ZC, ZS, ZW). Both bare root symbols (ES → continuous front-month via CONTFUT) and full contract tickers (ESH6 → March 2026 specific contract) are supported. Exchange routing uses the same mapping as the trading engine. An amber "IB" badge appears next to the ticker when chart data comes from Interactive Brokers. Agent v1.19.6.

features.title: Watchlist Quote Reliability Fix
features.summary: Fixed batch quote fetching that was overwhelming the IB agent and causing complete data loss
features.description:
The watchlist's batch quote fetcher was sending all 18 ticker requests concurrently to the IB agent, which processes them sequentially. This caused WebSocket disconnections and prevented any data from reaching the UI.

Three fixes:
1. Chunked batch requests (max 4 concurrent) to prevent agent overload
2. Per-request 10-second timeout via AbortSignal.timeout to bound total fetch time
3. Overlap guard in the polling hook to prevent cascading fetches

Also fixed monthly commodity futures front-month derivation — CL/NG/GC/SI contracts expire weeks before the delivery month, so the agent now uses the next month for monthly symbols. Silver (SI) contracts are disambiguated with the standard 5000oz multiplier. Agent v1.19.6.
